name: Recursion
description: Questions on recursion, base cases, and call stacks.

questions.question: A recursive function must have:
questions.explanation: A base case stops the recursion; without it you get infinite recursion and stack overflow.

questions.question: What is the maximum recursion depth in Python (typical default)?
questions.explanation: sys.getrecursionlimit() is typically 1000; you can change it with setrecursionlimit().

questions.question: Recursive factorial: def f(n): return 1 if n <= 1 else n * f(n-1). What is f(0)?
questions.explanation: Base case n <= 1 returns 1, so f(0) and f(1) both return 1.

questions.question: Tail recursion in Python is:
questions.explanation: Python does not optimize tail recursion; each call uses stack space.

questions.question: Which problem is naturally recursive?
choices: Computing Fibonacci F(n) from F(n-1) and F(n-2)
questions.explanation: Fibonacci is defined recursively; tree traversal and divide-and-conquer are other natural fits.
